  - Archive: `[{"id":"source-bayesian-networks","type":{"zh":"论文","en":"Paper"},"label":{"zh":"贝叶斯网络","en":"Bayesian Networks"},"url":"https://ftp.cs.ucla.edu/pub/stat_ser/R246.pdf","reliability":"primary","sourceType":"paper"},{"id":"source-judea-pearl-homepage","type":{"zh":"个人主页","en":"Homepage"},"label":{"zh":"朱迪亚·珀尔 homepage","en":"Judea Pearl homepage"},"url":"https://bayes.cs.ucla.edu/home.htm","reliability":"reference-only","sourceType":"reference-only"},{"id":"source-judea-pearl-ucla-samueli","type":{"zh":"人物资料","en":"Profile"},"label":{"zh":"朱迪亚·珀尔, 加州大学洛杉矶分校 Samueli","en":"Judea Pearl, UCLA Samueli"},"url":"https://samueli.ucla.edu/judea-pearl/","reliability":"reference-only","sourceType":"reference-only"}]`
- `commentarySections`
  - Legacy: `[{"label":{"en":"Historical Background","zh":"历史背景"},"html":{"en":"Symbolic AI struggled with uncertainty. Probabilistic graphical models connected knowledge representation with statistical inference.","zh":"符号 AI 难以处理不确定性。概率图模型把知识表示与统计推断连接起来。"}},{"label":{"en":"Core Idea","zh":"核心思想"},"html":{"en":"A Bayesian network factorizes a joint distribution according to a directed acyclic graph, making inference modular. The key mechanism is Directed acyclic graph plus conditional probabilities, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","zh":"贝叶斯网络按有向无环图分解联合分布，使推断模块化。 关键机制是有向无环图加条件概率，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。"}},{"label":{"en":"Long-Term Legacy","zh":"长期影响"},"html":{"en":"Experts generally treat Bayesian networks as a foundational language for uncertain reasoning. Their long-term legacy is the graphical connection between probability, evidence, and dependency structure, which later influenced causal reasoning and decision support.","zh":"专家通常把贝叶斯网络视为不确定推理的基础语言。它的长期影响在于用图结构连接概率、证据和依赖关系，并影响了后来的因果推理和决策支持。"}}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"historical-background","label":{"zh":"历史背景","en":"Historical Background"},"html":{"zh":"符号 AI 难以处理不确定性。概率图模型把知识表示与统计推断连接起来。","en":"Symbolic AI struggled with uncertainty. Probabilistic graphical models connected knowledge representation with statistical inference."},"sourceIds":["source-bayesian-networks"]},{"id":"core-idea","label":{"zh":"核心思想","en":"Core Idea"},"html":{"zh":"贝叶斯网络按有向无环图分解联合分布，使推断模块化。 关键机制是有向无环图加条件概率，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","en":"A Bayesian network factorizes a joint distribution according to a directed acyclic graph, making inference modular. The key mechanism is Directed acyclic graph plus conditional probabilities, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior."},"sourceIds":["source-bayesian-networks"]},{"id":"long-term-legacy","label":{"zh":"长期影响","en":"Long-Term Legacy"},"html":{"zh":"专家通常把贝叶斯网络视为不确定推理的基础语言。它的长期影响在于用图结构连接概率、证据和依赖关系，并影响了后来的因果推理和决策支持。","en":"Experts generally treat Bayesian networks as a foundational language for uncertain reasoning. Their long-term legacy is the graphical connection between probability, evidence, and dependency structure, which later influenced causal reasoning and decision support."},"sourceIds":["source-bayesian-networks"]}]`
- `quizzes`
  - Legacy: `[{"id":"1985-bayesian-network-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What does a Bayesian network combine?","zh":"贝叶斯网络结合了什么？"},"options":[{"en":"A directed graph and conditional probabilities","zh":"有向图与条件概率"},{"en":"A chess chip and opening book only","zh":"只有棋类芯片和开局库"},{"en":"A scale-space image descriptor only","zh":"只有尺度空间图像描述子"},{"en":"A blocks-world parser without uncertainty","zh":"没有不确定性的积木世界解析器"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"The graph expresses dependency structure, and the probabilities support belief updating under evidence.","zh":"图表达依赖结构，概率支持在证据下更新信念。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["probabilistic-ai","knowledge-representation"]}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"1985-bayesian-network-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"贝叶斯网络结合了什么？","en":"What does a Bayesian network combine?"},"options":[{"zh":"有向图与条件概率","en":"A directed graph and conditional probabilities"},{"zh":"只有棋类芯片和开局库","en":"A chess chip and opening book only"},{"zh":"只有尺度空间图像描述子","en":"A scale-space image descriptor only"},{"zh":"没有不确定性的积木世界解析器","en":"A blocks-world parser without uncertainty"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"图表达依赖结构，概率支持在证据下更新信念。","en":"The graph expresses dependency structure, and the probabilities support belief updating under evidence."},"sourceIds":["source-bayesian-networks"],"assetIds":["asset-1985-bayesian-network-judea-pearl"]}]`
- `viewModel.zh`
  - Legacy: `{"year":"1985","title":"贝叶斯网络","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就","subtitle":"AI 百大成就","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>贝叶斯网络为 AI 提供了一种紧凑的不确定推理语言。有向图编码变量之间的依赖关系，条件概率表量化证据如何改变信念。这个思想成为诊断、传感器融合、决策支持和后来的因果建模的核心。</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/photos/1985-bayesian-network_judea-pearl.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1985-bayesian-network_dag.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1985-bayesian-network_message-pass.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"有向无环图加条件概率\"","quoteAttribution":"《贝叶斯网络技术论文》, 朱迪亚·珀尔","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100：概率图模型 / 贝叶斯网络","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"有向无环图加条件概率\"<br>source：《贝叶斯网络技术论文》, 朱迪亚·珀尔"},{"label":"历史背景","html":"符号 AI 难以处理不确定性。概率图模型把知识表示与统计推断连接起来。","link":null},{"label":"核心思想","html":"贝叶斯网络按有向无环图分解联合分布，使推断模块化。 关键机制是有向无环图加条件概率，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","link":null},{"label":"长期影响","html":"专家通常把贝叶斯网络视为不确定推理的基础语言。它的长期影响在于用图结构连接概率、证据和依赖关系，并影响了后来的因果推理和决策支持。","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1985-bayesian-network-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What does a Bayesian network combine?","zh":"贝叶斯网络结合了什么？"},"options":[{"en":"A directed graph and conditional probabilities","zh":"有向图与条件概率"},{"en":"A chess chip and opening book only","zh":"只有棋类芯片和开局库"},{"en":"A scale-space image descriptor only","zh":"只有尺度空间图像描述子"},{"en":"A blocks-world parser without uncertainty","zh":"没有不确定性的积木世界解析器"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"The graph expresses dependency structure, and the probabilities support belief updating under evidence.","zh":"图表达依赖结构，概率支持在证据下更新信念。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["probabilistic-ai","knowledge-representation"]}]}`
  - Archive: `{"year":"1985","title":"贝叶斯网络","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就","subtitle":"AI 百大成就","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>贝叶斯网络为 AI 提供了一种紧凑的不确定推理语言。有向图编码变量之间的依赖关系，条件概率表量化证据如何改变信念。这个思想成为诊断、传感器融合、决策支持和后来的因果建模的核心。</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/photos/1985-bayesian-network_judea-pearl.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1985-bayesian-network_dag.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1985-bayesian-network_message-pass.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"有向无环图加条件概率\"","quoteAttribution":"《贝叶斯网络技术论文》, 朱迪亚·珀尔","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100：概率图模型 / 贝叶斯网络","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"有向无环图加条件概率\"<br>source：《贝叶斯网络技术论文》, 朱迪亚·珀尔"},{"label":"历史背景","html":"符号 AI 难以处理不确定性。概率图模型把知识表示与统计推断连接起来。","link":null},{"label":"核心思想","html":"贝叶斯网络按有向无环图分解联合分布，使推断模块化。 关键机制是有向无环图加条件概率，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","link":null},{"label":"长期影响","html":"专家通常把贝叶斯网络视为不确定推理的基础语言。它的长期影响在于用图结构连接概率、证据和依赖关系，并影响了后来的因果推理和决策支持。","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1985-bayesian-network-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"贝叶斯网络结合了什么？","en":"What does a Bayesian network combine?"},"options":[{"zh":"有向图与条件概率","en":"A directed graph and conditional probabilities"},{"zh":"只有棋类芯片和开局库","en":"A chess chip and opening book only"},{"zh":"只有尺度空间图像描述子","en":"A scale-space image descriptor only"},{"zh":"没有不确定性的积木世界解析器","en":"A blocks-world parser without uncertainty"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"图表达依赖结构，概率支持在证据下更新信念。","en":"The graph expresses dependency structure, and the probabilities support belief updating under evidence."},"sourceIds":["source-bayesian-networks"],"assetIds":["asset-1985-bayesian-network-judea-pearl"]}]}`
- `viewModel.en`
  - Legacy: `{"year":"1985","title":"Bayesian Network","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 Achievements","subtitle":"AI Top 100 Achievements","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>Bayesian networks gave AI a compact language for uncertain reasoning. A directed graph encodes dependencies among variables, while conditional probability tables quantify how evidence should change beliefs. The idea became central to diagnosis, sensor fusion, decision support, and later causal modeling.</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/photos/1985-bayesian-network_judea-pearl.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1985-bayesian-network_dag.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1985-bayesian-network_message-pass.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"Directed acyclic graph plus conditional probabilities\"","quoteAttribution":"<em>Bayesian Networks technical paper</em>, Judea Pearl","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100: Probabilistic graphical models / Bayesian Network","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"Directed acyclic graph plus conditional probabilities\"<br>source：<em>Bayesian Networks technical paper</em>, Judea Pearl"},{"label":"Historical Background","html":"Symbolic AI struggled with uncertainty. Probabilistic graphical models connected knowledge representation with statistical inference.","link":null},{"label":"Core Idea","html":"A Bayesian network factorizes a joint distribution according to a directed acyclic graph, making inference modular. The key mechanism is Directed acyclic graph plus conditional probabilities, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","link":null},{"label":"Long-Term Legacy","html":"Experts generally treat Bayesian networks as a foundational language for uncertain reasoning. Their long-term legacy is the graphical connection between probability, evidence, and dependency structure, which later influenced causal reasoning and decision support.","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1985-bayesian-network-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What does a Bayesian network combine?","zh":"贝叶斯网络结合了什么？"},"options":[{"en":"A directed graph and conditional probabilities","zh":"有向图与条件概率"},{"en":"A chess chip and opening book only","zh":"只有棋类芯片和开局库"},{"en":"A scale-space image descriptor only","zh":"只有尺度空间图像描述子"},{"en":"A blocks-world parser without uncertainty","zh":"没有不确定性的积木世界解析器"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"The graph expresses dependency structure, and the probabilities support belief updating under evidence.","zh":"图表达依赖结构，概率支持在证据下更新信念。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["probabilistic-ai","knowledge-representation"]}]}`
  - Archive: `{"year":"1985","title":"Bayesian Network","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 Achievements","subtitle":"AI Top 100 Achievements","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>Bayesian networks gave AI a compact language for uncertain reasoning. The key mechanism is Directed acyclic graph plus conditional probabilities, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","link":null},{"label":"Long-Term Legacy","html":"Experts generally treat Bayesian networks as a foundational language for uncertain reasoning. - `commentarySections`
  - Legacy: `[{"label":{"en":"Background","zh":"背景解读"},"html":{"en":"The key to backpropagation was not merely sending errors backward. It embedded every layer's parameters in a unified differentiable computation graph, making representation learning a genuinely trainable engineering process for the first time.","zh":"反向传播的关键不只是“把误差往回传”，而是把多层网络中每一层参数都嵌入统一的可微计算图里，让表示学习第一次变成真正可训练的工程流程。"}},{"label":{"en":"Context","zh":"延展说明"},"html":{"en":"Its foundation for the later deep-learning revival was to turn the question of what hidden layers can learn from a hand-design problem into a data-driven one. That step moved neural networks from conceptual models toward a scalable methodology.","zh":"它为后来深度学习复兴打下的基础，是让“隐藏层能学到什么”从人工设计问题变成数据驱动问题；这一步把神经网络从概念模型推进到了可扩展的方法论。"}}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"background","label":{"zh":"背景解读","en":"Background"},"html":{"zh":"反向传播的关键不只是“把误差往回传”，而是把多层网络中每一层参数都嵌入统一的可微计算图里，让表示学习第一次变成真正可训练的工程流程。","en":"The key to backpropagation was not merely sending errors backward. It embedded every layer's parameters in a unified differentiable computation graph, making representation learning a genuinely trainable engineering process for the first time."},"sourceIds":["source-legacy-event-record"]},{"id":"context","label":{"zh":"延展说明","en":"Context"},"html":{"zh":"它为后来深度学习复兴打下的基础，是让“隐藏层能学到什么”从人工设计问题变成数据驱动问题；这一步把神经网络从概念模型推进到了可扩展的方法论。","en":"Its foundation for the later deep-learning revival was to turn the question of what hidden layers can learn from a hand-design problem into a data-driven one. That step moved neural networks from conceptual models toward a scalable methodology."},"sourceIds":["source-legacy-event-record"]}]`

### milestone-1986-rnn

- Kind: changed
- `date`
  - Legacy: `null`
  - Archive: `"1986"`
- `achievement.visualModules`
  - Legacy: `null`
  - Archive: `[]`
- `achievement.sources`
  - Legacy: `null`
  - Archive: `[]`
- `commentarySections`
  - Legacy: `[{"label":{"en":"Background","zh":"背景解读"},"html":{"en":"The breakthrough in early RNNs was putting historical state explicitly back inside the network. Context units let the model look beyond the current input and compress earlier time steps into an internal memory it could read repeatedly.","zh":"早期 RNN 的突破点在于把“历史状态”显式放回网络内部：context units 让模型不再只看当前输入，而是能把先前时间步的信息压缩成一个可反复读取的内部记忆。"}},{"label":{"en":"Context","zh":"延展说明"},"html":{"en":"They also exposed a long-running problem: sequence modeling was an early idea, but without stable training mechanisms these models struggled to learn long-range dependencies. That line of work was only truly reconnected later through LSTM.","zh":"它也暴露了一个长期难题：序列建模的想法很早就有，但没有稳定训练机制时，这类模型很难真正把长距离依赖学出来；这条线最后要靠 LSTM 才被接上。"}}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"background","label":{"zh":"背景解读","en":"Background"},"html":{"zh":"早期 RNN 的突破点在于把“历史状态”显式放回网络内部：context units 让模型不再只看当前输入，而是能把先前时间步的信息压缩成一个可反复读取的内部记忆。","en":"The breakthrough in early RNNs was putting historical state explicitly back inside the network. Context units let the model look beyond the current input and compress earlier time steps into an internal memory it could read repeatedly."},"sourceIds":["source-legacy-event-record"]},{"id":"context","label":{"zh":"延展说明","en":"Context"},"html":{"zh":"它也暴露了一个长期难题：序列建模的想法很早就有，但没有稳定训练机制时，这类模型很难真正把长距离依赖学出来；这条线最后要靠 LSTM 才被接上。","en":"They also exposed a long-running problem: sequence modeling was an early idea, but without stable training mechanisms these models struggled to learn long-range dependencies. That line of work was only truly reconnected later through LSTM."},"sourceIds":["source-legacy-event-record"]}]`
- `quizzes`
  - Legacy: `null`
  - Archive: `[]`

### milestone-1988-td-update

- Kind: changed
- `date`
  - Legacy: `null`
  - Archive: `"1988"`
- `achievement.sources`
  - Legacy: `[{"type":{"en":"Paper","zh":"论文"},"label":{"en":"Learning to Predict by the Methods of Temporal Differences","zh":"Learning to Predict by the Methods of Temporal Differences"},"url":"https://doi.org/10.1007/BF00115009"},{"type":{"en":"Homepage","zh":"个人主页"},"label":{"en":"Richard Sutton homepage","zh":"理查德·萨顿 homepage"},"url":"http://incompleteideas.net/"},{"type":{"en":"Award","zh":"奖项资料"},"label":{"en":"ACM 2024 Turing Award announcement","zh":"ACM 2024 图灵 Award announcement"},"url":"https://awards.acm.org/about/2024-turing"}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"source-learning-to-predict-by-the-methods-of-temporal-differences","type":{"zh":"论文","en":"Paper"},"label":{"zh":"Learning to Predict by the Methods of Temporal Differences","en":"Learning to Predict by the Methods of Temporal Differences"},"url":"https://doi.org/10.1007/BF00115009","reliability":"primary","sourceType":"paper"},{"id":"source-richard-sutton-homepage","type":{"zh":"个人主页","en":"Homepage"},"label":{"zh":"理查德·萨顿 homepage","en":"Richard Sutton homepage"},"url":"http://incompleteideas.net/","reliability":"reference-only","sourceType":"reference-only"},{"id":"source-acm-2024-turing-award-announcement","type":{"zh":"奖项资料","en":"Award"},"label":{"zh":"ACM 2024 图灵 Award announcement","en":"ACM 2024 Turing Award announcement"},"url":"https://awards.acm.org/about/2024-turing","reliability":"reference-only","sourceType":"reference-only"}]`
- `commentarySections`
  - Legacy: `[{"label":{"en":"Historical Background","zh":"历史背景"},"html":{"en":"Learning from delayed rewards required a way to assign credit before the entire episode ended. This context helps viewers place Temporal-Difference Update in the technical problems and research priorities of its time.","zh":"从延迟奖励中学习需要一种在整个回合结束前分配信用的方法。 这段背景帮助观众把时序差分更新放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。"}},{"label":{"en":"Core Idea","zh":"核心思想"},"html":{"en":"TD compares the current prediction with a reward plus the next prediction, then nudges the current value toward that target. The key mechanism is Bootstrapped value prediction, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","zh":"TD 比较当前预测与“奖励加下一步预测”，再把当前价值向该目标推进。 关键机制是自举式价值预测，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。"}},{"label":{"en":"Long-Term Legacy","zh":"长期影响"},"html":{"en":"Experts generally treat temporal-difference learning as a cornerstone of reinforcement learning. Its long-term legacy is the bootstrapping idea that value estimates can learn from later predictions, supporting Q-learning, actor-critic methods, and modern value-based control.","zh":"专家通常把时序差分学习视为强化学习的基石。它的长期影响在于提出价值估计可以从后续预测中自举学习，并支撑了 Q-learning、actor-critic 方法和现代基于价值的控制。"}}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"historical-background","label":{"zh":"历史背景","en":"Historical Background"},"html":{"zh":"从延迟奖励中学习需要一种在整个回合结束前分配信用的方法。 这段背景帮助观众把时序差分更新放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。","en":"Learning from delayed rewards required a way to assign credit before the entire episode ended. This context helps viewers place Temporal-Difference Update in the technical problems and research priorities of its time."},"sourceIds":["source-learning-to-predict-by-the-methods-of-temporal-differences"]},{"id":"core-idea","label":{"zh":"核心思想","en":"Core Idea"},"html":{"zh":"TD 比较当前预测与“奖励加下一步预测”，再把当前价值向该目标推进。 关键机制是自举式价值预测，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","en":"TD compares the current prediction with a reward plus the next prediction, then nudges the current value toward that target. The key mechanism is Bootstrapped value prediction, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior."},"sourceIds":["source-learning-to-predict-by-the-methods-of-temporal-differences"]},{"id":"long-term-legacy","label":{"zh":"长期影响","en":"Long-Term Legacy"},"html":{"zh":"专家通常把时序差分学习视为强化学习的基石。它的长期影响在于提出价值估计可以从后续预测中自举学习，并支撑了 Q-learning、actor-critic 方法和现代基于价值的控制。","en":"Experts generally treat temporal-difference learning as a cornerstone of reinforcement learning. Its long-term legacy is the bootstrapping idea that value estimates can learn from later predictions, supporting Q-learning, actor-critic methods, and modern value-based control."},"sourceIds":["source-learning-to-predict-by-the-methods-of-temporal-differences"]}]`
- `quizzes`
  - Legacy: `[{"id":"1988-td-update-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What does a TD update compare?","zh":"TD 更新比较什么？"},"options":[{"en":"The current value estimate with reward plus next value estimate","zh":"当前价值估计与“奖励加下一价值估计”"},{"en":"Two unrelated image captions","zh":"两个无关图像标题"},{"en":"A molecule with a chess position","zh":"一个分子与一个棋局"},{"en":"Only final episode reward after all learning stops","zh":"学习停止后才看的最终回合奖励"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"TD learning bootstraps by using the next prediction as part of the learning target.","zh":"TD 学习用下一步预测作为学习目标的一部分来进行自举。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["reinforcement-learning","value"]}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"1988-td-update-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"TD 更新比较什么？","en":"What does a TD update compare?"},"options":[{"zh":"当前价值估计与“奖励加下一价值估计”","en":"The current value estimate with reward plus next value estimate"},{"zh":"两个无关图像标题","en":"Two unrelated image captions"},{"zh":"一个分子与一个棋局","en":"A molecule with a chess position"},{"zh":"学习停止后才看的最终回合奖励","en":"Only final episode reward after all learning stops"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"TD 学习用下一步预测作为学习目标的一部分来进行自举。","en":"TD learning bootstraps by using the next prediction as part of the learning target."},"sourceIds":["source-learning-to-predict-by-the-methods-of-temporal-differences"],"assetIds":["asset-1988-td-update-richard-sutton"]}]`
- `viewModel.zh`
  - Legacy: `{"year":"1988","title":"时序差分更新","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就","subtitle":"AI 百大成就","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>时序差分学习为强化学习提供了一种从经验中学习预测的紧凑方法。TD 方法不必等到最终结果，而是用下一步奖励和下一步预测来更新价值估计。这种自举思想支撑了 TD-Gammon、Q-learning 和 actor-critic 等后续算法。</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/photos/1988-td-update_richard-sutton.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1988-td-update_value-timeline.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1988-td-update_error-meter.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"自举式价值预测\"","quoteAttribution":"《通过时序差分学习预测》, 理查德·萨顿","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100：强化学习 / 时序差分更新","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"自举式价值预测\"<br>source：《通过时序差分学习预测》, 理查德·萨顿"},{"label":"历史背景","html":"从延迟奖励中学习需要一种在整个回合结束前分配信用的方法。 这段背景帮助观众把时序差分更新放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。","link":null},{"label":"核心思想","html":"TD 比较当前预测与“奖励加下一步预测”，再把当前价值向该目标推进。 关键机制是自举式价值预测，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","link":null},{"label":"长期影响","html":"专家通常把时序差分学习视为强化学习的基石。它的长期影响在于提出价值估计可以从后续预测中自举学习，并支撑了 Q-learning、actor-critic 方法和现代基于价值的控制。","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1988-td-update-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What does a TD update compare?","zh":"TD 更新比较什么？"},"options":[{"en":"The current value estimate with reward plus next value estimate","zh":"当前价值估计与“奖励加下一价值估计”"},{"en":"Two unrelated image captions","zh":"两个无关图像标题"},{"en":"A molecule with a chess position","zh":"一个分子与一个棋局"},{"en":"Only final episode reward after all learning stops","zh":"学习停止后才看的最终回合奖励"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"TD learning bootstraps by using the next prediction as part of the learning target.","zh":"TD 学习用下一步预测作为学习目标的一部分来进行自举。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["reinforcement-learning","value"]}]}`
  - Archive: `{"year":"1988","title":"时序差分更新","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就","subtitle":"AI 百大成就","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>时序差分学习为强化学习提供了一种从经验中学习预测的紧凑方法。TD 方法不必等到最终结果，而是用下一步奖励和下一步预测来更新价值估计。这种自举思想支撑了 TD-Gammon、Q-learning 和 actor-critic 等后续算法。</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/photos/1988-td-update_richard-sutton.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1988-td-update_value-timeline.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1988-td-update_error-meter.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"自举式价值预测\"","quoteAttribution":"《通过时序差分学习预测》, 理查德·萨顿","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100：强化学习 / 时序差分更新","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"自举式价值预测\"<br>source：《通过时序差分学习预测》, 理查德·萨顿"},{"label":"历史背景","html":"从延迟奖励中学习需要一种在整个回合结束前分配信用的方法。 这段背景帮助观众把时序差分更新放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。","link":null},{"label":"核心思想","html":"TD 比较当前预测与“奖励加下一步预测”，再把当前价值向该目标推进。 关键机制是自举式价值预测，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","link":null},{"label":"长期影响","html":"专家通常把时序差分学习视为强化学习的基石。它的长期影响在于提出价值估计可以从后续预测中自举学习，并支撑了 Q-learning、actor-critic 方法和现代基于价值的控制。","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1988-td-update-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"TD 更新比较什么？","en":"What does a TD update compare?"},"options":[{"zh":"当前价值估计与“奖励加下一价值估计”","en":"The current value estimate with reward plus next value estimate"},{"zh":"两个无关图像标题","en":"Two unrelated image captions"},{"zh":"一个分子与一个棋局","en":"A molecule with a chess position"},{"zh":"学习停止后才看的最终回合奖励","en":"Only final episode reward after all learning stops"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"TD 学习用下一步预测作为学习目标的一部分来进行自举。","en":"TD learning bootstraps by using the next prediction as part of the learning target."},"sourceIds":["source-learning-to-predict-by-the-methods-of-temporal-differences"],"assetIds":["asset-1988-td-update-richard-sutton"]}]}`
- `viewModel.en`
  - Legacy: `{"year":"1988","title":"Temporal-Difference Update","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 Achievements","subtitle":"AI Top 100 Achievements","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>Temporal-difference learning gave reinforcement learning a compact way to learn predictions from experience. Instead of waiting for a final outcome, TD methods update value estimates using the next reward and the next prediction. This bootstrapping idea sits behind many later algorithms, including TD-Gammon, Q-learning, and actor-critic methods.</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/photos/1988-td-update_richard-sutton.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1988-td-update_value-timeline.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1988-td-update_error-meter.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"Bootstrapped value prediction\"","quoteAttribution":"<em>Learning to predict by temporal differences</em>, Richard Sutton","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100: Reinforcement learning / Temporal-Difference Update","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"Bootstrapped value prediction\"<br>source：<em>Learning to predict by temporal differences</em>, Richard Sutton"},{"label":"Historical Background","html":"Learning from delayed rewards required a way to assign credit before the entire episode ended. This context helps viewers place Temporal-Difference Update in the technical problems and research priorities of its time.","link":null},{"label":"Core Idea","html":"TD compares the current prediction with a reward plus the next prediction, then nudges the current value toward that target. The key mechanism is Bootstrapped value prediction, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","link":null},{"label":"Long-Term Legacy","html":"Experts generally treat temporal-difference learning as a cornerstone of reinforcement learning. Its long-term legacy is the bootstrapping idea that value estimates can learn from later predictions, supporting Q-learning, actor-critic methods, and modern value-based control.","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1988-td-update-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What does a TD update compare?","zh":"TD 更新比较什么？"},"options":[{"en":"The current value estimate with reward plus next value estimate","zh":"当前价值估计与“奖励加下一价值估计”"},{"en":"Two unrelated image captions","zh":"两个无关图像标题"},{"en":"A molecule with a chess position","zh":"一个分子与一个棋局"},{"en":"Only final episode reward after all learning stops","zh":"学习停止后才看的最终回合奖励"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"TD learning bootstraps by using the next prediction as part of the learning target.","zh":"TD 学习用下一步预测作为学习目标的一部分来进行自举。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["reinforcement-learning","value"]}]}`
  - Archive: `{"year":"1988","title":"Temporal-Difference Update","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 Achievements","subtitle":"AI Top 100 Achievements","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>Temporal-difference learning gave reinforcement learning a compact way to learn predictions from experience. Instead of waiting for a final outcome, TD methods update value estimates using the next reward and the next prediction. This bootstrapping idea sits behind many later algorithms, including TD-Gammon, Q-learning, and actor-critic methods.</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/photos/1988-td-update_richard-sutton.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1988-td-update_value-timeline.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1988-td-update_error-meter.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"Bootstrapped value prediction\"","quoteAttribution":"<em>Learning to predict by temporal differences</em>, Richard Sutton","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100: Reinforcement learning / Temporal-Difference Update","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"Bootstrapped value prediction\"<br>source：<em>Learning to predict by temporal differences</em>, Richard Sutton"},{"label":"Historical Background","html":"Learning from delayed rewards required a way to assign credit before the entire episode ended. This context helps viewers place Temporal-Difference Update in the technical problems and research priorities of its time.","link":null},{"label":"Core Idea","html":"TD compares the current prediction with a reward plus the next prediction, then nudges the current value toward that target. The key mechanism is Bootstrapped value prediction, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","link":null},{"label":"Long-Term Legacy","html":"Experts generally treat temporal-difference learning as a cornerstone of reinforcement learning. Its long-term legacy is the bootstrapping idea that value estimates can learn from later predictions, supporting Q-learning, actor-critic methods, and modern value-based control.","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1988-td-update-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"TD 更新比较什么？","en":"What does a TD update compare?"},"options":[{"zh":"当前价值估计与“奖励加下一价值估计”","en":"The current value estimate with reward plus next value estimate"},{"zh":"两个无关图像标题","en":"Two unrelated image captions"},{"zh":"一个分子与一个棋局","en":"A molecule with a chess position"},{"zh":"学习停止后才看的最终回合奖励","en":"Only final episode reward after all learning stops"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"TD 学习用下一步预测作为学习目标的一部分来进行自举。","en":"TD learning bootstraps by using the next prediction as part of the learning target."},"sourceIds":["source-learning-to-predict-by-the-methods-of-temporal-differences"],"assetIds":["asset-1988-td-update-richard-sutton"]}]}`

- `commentarySections`
  - Legacy: `[{"label":{"en":"Historical Background","zh":"历史背景"},"html":{"en":"Yann LeCun and collaborators showed that convolutional networks trained by backpropagation could read handwritten ZIP codes. LeNet became a defining example of end-to-end convolutional perception.","zh":"扬·勒昆及合作者证明，用反向传播训练的卷积网络可以识别手写邮编。LeNet 成为端到端卷积感知系统的标志性案例。"}},{"label":{"en":"Core Idea","zh":"核心思想"},"html":{"en":"Convolutions extract local strokes, pooling stabilizes them, and a classifier predicts digits. The interactive demo focuses on the steps that connect the source material to the visible system behavior.","zh":"卷积提取局部笔画，池化稳定特征，分类器预测数字。 互动演示会突出这些步骤如何把资料线索与可见的系统行为连接起来。"}},{"label":{"en":"Long-Term Legacy","zh":"长期影响"},"html":{"en":"Experts generally treat LeNet as a canonical early proof that convolution, weight sharing, and backpropagation could solve real perception tasks. Its long-term legacy is the end-to-end CNN pattern that later scaled into modern computer vision.","zh":"专家通常把 LeNet 视为早期经典证据，证明卷积、权重共享和反向传播可以解决真实感知任务。它的长期影响在于端到端 CNN 模式后来扩展成现代计算机视觉的基础。"}}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"historical-background","label":{"zh":"历史背景","en":"Historical Background"},"html":{"zh":"扬·勒昆及合作者证明，用反向传播训练的卷积网络可以识别手写邮编。LeNet 成为端到端卷积感知系统的标志性案例。","en":"Yann LeCun and collaborators showed that convolutional networks trained by backpropagation could read handwritten ZIP codes. LeNet became a defining example of end-to-end convolutional perception."},"sourceIds":["source-backpropagation-applied-to-handwritten-zip-code-recognition"]},{"id":"core-idea","label":{"zh":"核心思想","en":"Core Idea"},"html":{"zh":"卷积提取局部笔画，池化稳定特征，分类器预测数字。 互动演示会突出这些步骤如何把资料线索与可见的系统行为连接起来。","en":"Convolutions extract local strokes, pooling stabilizes them, and a classifier predicts digits. The interactive demo focuses on the steps that connect the source material to the visible system behavior."},"sourceIds":["source-backpropagation-applied-to-handwritten-zip-code-recognition"]},{"id":"long-term-legacy","label":{"zh":"长期影响","en":"Long-Term Legacy"},"html":{"zh":"专家通常把 LeNet 视为早期经典证据，证明卷积、权重共享和反向传播可以解决真实感知任务。它的长期影响在于端到端 CNN 模式后来扩展成现代计算机视觉的基础。","en":"Experts generally treat LeNet as a canonical early proof that convolution, weight sharing, and backpropagation could solve real perception tasks. Its long-term legacy is the end-to-end CNN pattern that later scaled into modern computer vision."},"sourceIds":["source-backpropagation-applied-to-handwritten-zip-code-recognition"]}]`

### milestone-1990-otter

- Kind: changed
- `date`
  - Legacy: `null`
  - Archive: `"1990s"`
- `achievement.sources`
  - Legacy: `[{"type":{"en":"Reference","zh":"参考文献"},"label":{"en":"OTTER 3.3 Reference Manual","zh":"OTTER 3.3 参考手册"},"url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/cs/0310056"},{"type":{"en":"Paper","zh":"论文"},"label":{"en":"A Spectrum of Applications of Automated Reasoning","zh":"自动推理应用谱系"},"url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/cs/0205078"},{"type":{"en":"Application paper","zh":"应用论文"},"label":{"en":"Checking Clinical Guidelines using Automated Reasoning Tools","zh":"使用自动推理工具检查临床指南"},"url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/0806.0250"}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"source-otter-3-3-reference-manual","type":{"zh":"参考文献","en":"Reference"},"label":{"zh":"OTTER 3.3 参考手册","en":"OTTER 3.3 Reference Manual"},"url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/cs/0310056","reliability":"reference-only","sourceType":"reference-only"},{"id":"source-a-spectrum-of-applications-of-automated-reasoning","type":{"zh":"论文","en":"Paper"},"label":{"zh":"自动推理应用谱系","en":"A Spectrum of Applications of Automated Reasoning"},"url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/cs/0205078","reliability":"secondary","sourceType":"paper"},{"id":"source-checking-clinical-guidelines-using-automated-reasoning-tools","type":{"zh":"应用论文","en":"Application paper"},"label":{"zh":"使用自动推理工具检查临床指南","en":"Checking Clinical Guidelines using Automated Reasoning Tools"},"url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/0806.0250","reliability":"secondary","sourceType":"paper"}]`
- `commentarySections`
  - Legacy: `[{"label":{"en":"Historical Background","zh":"历史背景"},"html":{"en":"Automated reasoning systems had powerful inference rules but often struggled with search explosion. Otter focused on practical control of that search.","zh":"自动推理系统拥有强大的推理规则，但常常受困于搜索爆炸。Otter 的重点是以工程化方式控制这种搜索。"}},{"label":{"en":"Core Idea","zh":"核心思想"},"html":{"en":"The prover repeatedly selects a promising clause, generates consequences through inference rules, and uses indexing and simplification to keep the search tractable. The key mechanism is Given-clause resolution and paramodulation, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","zh":"证明器反复选择有希望的子句，用推理规则生成后果，并通过索引和化简让搜索保持可控。 关键机制是given-clause 归结与参数调解，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。"}},{"label":{"en":"Long-Term Legacy","zh":"长期影响"},"html":{"en":"Experts generally treat Otter as a workhorse system in automated theorem proving and formal reasoning. Its long-term legacy is practical given-clause search, equality reasoning, and proof automation that influenced later tools including Prover9.","zh":"专家通常把 Otter 视为自动定理证明和形式推理中的主力系统。它的长期影响在于实用的 given-clause 搜索、等式推理和证明自动化，并影响了包括 Prover9 在内的后续工具。"}}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"historical-background","label":{"zh":"历史背景","en":"Historical Background"},"html":{"zh":"自动推理系统拥有强大的推理规则，但常常受困于搜索爆炸。Otter 的重点是以工程化方式控制这种搜索。","en":"Automated reasoning systems had powerful inference rules but often struggled with search explosion. Otter focused on practical control of that search."},"sourceIds":["source-otter-3-3-reference-manual"]},{"id":"core-idea","label":{"zh":"核心思想","en":"Core Idea"},"html":{"zh":"证明器反复选择有希望的子句，用推理规则生成后果，并通过索引和化简让搜索保持可控。 关键机制是given-clause 归结与参数调解，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","en":"The prover repeatedly selects a promising clause, generates consequences through inference rules, and uses indexing and simplification to keep the search tractable. The key mechanism is Given-clause resolution and paramodulation, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior."},"sourceIds":["source-otter-3-3-reference-manual"]},{"id":"long-term-legacy","label":{"zh":"长期影响","en":"Long-Term Legacy"},"html":{"zh":"专家通常把 Otter 视为自动定理证明和形式推理中的主力系统。它的长期影响在于实用的 given-clause 搜索、等式推理和证明自动化，并影响了包括 Prover9 在内的后续工具。","en":"Experts generally treat Otter as a workhorse system in automated theorem proving and formal reasoning. Its long-term legacy is practical given-clause search, equality reasoning, and proof automation that influenced later tools including Prover9."},"sourceIds":["source-otter-3-3-reference-manual"]}]`
- `quizzes`
  - Legacy: `[{"id":"1990-otter-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What was central to Otter's practical theorem-proving style?","zh":"Otter 实用定理证明风格的核心是什么？"},"options":[{"en":"A controlled given-clause search with resolution and paramodulation","zh":"结合归结与参数调解的受控 given-clause 搜索"},{"en":"Only a fixed chess opening book","zh":"只有固定的棋类开局库"},{"en":"A neural image classifier","zh":"神经图像分类器"},{"en":"A random text generator","zh":"随机文本生成器"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"Otter searched first-order clause spaces using resolution-style inference, equality reasoning, indexing, and search-control heuristics.","zh":"Otter 用归结式推理、等式推理、索引和搜索控制启发式来搜索一阶子句空间。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["theorem-proving","symbolic-ai"]}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"1990-otter-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"Otter 实用定理证明风格的核心是什么？","en":"What was central to Otter's practical theorem-proving style?"},"options":[{"zh":"结合归结与参数调解的受控 given-clause 搜索","en":"A controlled given-clause search with resolution and paramodulation"},{"zh":"只有固定的棋类开局库","en":"Only a fixed chess opening book"},{"zh":"神经图像分类器","en":"A neural image classifier"},{"zh":"随机文本生成器","en":"A random text generator"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"Otter 用归结式推理、等式推理、索引和搜索控制启发式来搜索一阶子句空间。","en":"Otter searched first-order clause spaces using resolution-style inference, equality reasoning, indexing, and search-control heuristics."},"sourceIds":["source-otter-3-3-reference-manual"],"assetIds":["asset-1990-otter-input-clauses"]}]`
- `viewModel.zh`
  - Legacy: `{"year":"1990s","title":"Otter 自动定理证明器","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就","subtitle":"AI 百大成就","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>Otter 让高性能一阶定理证明成为 AI 与数理逻辑研究者可实际使用的工具。McCune 的系统结合归结、参数调解、项索引、权重控制和实用的 given-clause 搜索循环，成为探索大规模符号证明空间的主力工具。</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_input-clauses.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_proof-trace.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_given-clause.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_clause-index.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"让归结式证明搜索变得实用\"","quoteAttribution":"《OTTER 3.3 参考手册》, 威廉·麦丘恩","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100：自动定理证明 / Otter 自动定理证明器","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"让归结式证明搜索变得实用\"<br>source：《OTTER 3.3 参考手册》, 威廉·麦丘恩"},{"label":"历史背景","html":"自动推理系统拥有强大的推理规则，但常常受困于搜索爆炸。Otter 的重点是以工程化方式控制这种搜索。","link":null},{"label":"核心思想","html":"证明器反复选择有希望的子句，用推理规则生成后果，并通过索引和化简让搜索保持可控。 关键机制是given-clause 归结与参数调解，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","link":null},{"label":"长期影响","html":"专家通常把 Otter 视为自动定理证明和形式推理中的主力系统。它的长期影响在于实用的 given-clause 搜索、等式推理和证明自动化，并影响了包括 Prover9 在内的后续工具。","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1990-otter-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What was central to Otter's practical theorem-proving style?","zh":"Otter 实用定理证明风格的核心是什么？"},"options":[{"en":"A   - Legacy: `{"year":"1990s","title":"Otter","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 Achievements","subtitle":"AI Top 100 Achievements","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>Otter made high-performance first-order theorem proving widely usable for AI and mathematical logic researchers. McCune's system combined resolution, paramodulation, term indexing, weighting, and a practical given-clause search loop, becoming a workhorse for exploring large symbolic proof spaces.</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_input-clauses.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_proof-trace.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_given-clause.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_clause-index.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"Resolution-style proof search made practical\"","quoteAttribution":"<em>OTTER 3.3 Reference Manual</em>, William McCune","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100: Automated theorem proving / Otter","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"Resolution-style proof search made practical\"<br>source：<em>OTTER 3.3 Reference Manual</em>, William McCune"},{"label":"Historical Background","html":"Automated reasoning systems had powerful inference rules but often struggled with search explosion. Otter focused on practical control of that search.","link":null},{"label":"Core Idea","html":"The prover repeatedly selects a promising clause, generates consequences through inference rules, and uses indexing and simplification to keep the search tractable. The key mechanism is Given-clause resolution and paramodulation, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","link":null},{"label":"Long-Term Legacy","html":"Experts generally treat Otter as a workhorse system in automated theorem proving and formal reasoning. Its long-term legacy is practical given-clause search, equality reasoning, and proof automation that influenced later tools including Prover9.","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1990-otter-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What was central to Otter's practical theorem-proving style?","zh":"Otter 实用定理证明风格的核心是什么？"},"options":[{"en":"A controlled given-clause search with resolution and paramodulation","zh":"结合归结与参数调解的受控 given-clause 搜索"},{"en":"Only a fixed chess opening book","zh":"只有固定的棋类开局库"},{"en":"A neural image classifier","zh":"神经图像分类器"},{"en":"A random text generator","zh":"随机文本生成器"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"Otter searched first-order clause spaces using resolution-style inference, equality reasoning, indexing, and search-control heuristics.","zh":"Otter 用归结式推理、等式推理、索引和搜索控制启发式来搜索一阶子句空间。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["theorem-proving","symbolic-ai"]}]}`
  - Archive: `{"year":"1990s","title":"Otter","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 Achievements","subtitle":"AI Top 100 Achievements","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>Otter made high-performance first-order theorem proving widely usable for AI and mathematical logic researchers. McCune's system combined resolution, paramodulation, term indexing, weighting, and a practical given-clause search loop, becoming a workhorse for exploring large symbolic proof spaces.</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_input-clauses.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_proof-trace.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_given-clause.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1990-otter_clause-index.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"Resolution-style proof search made practical\"","quoteAttribution":"<em>OTTER 3.3 Reference Manual</em>, William McCune","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100: Automated theorem proving / Otter","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"Resolution-style proof search made practical\"<br>source：<em>OTTER 3.3 Reference Manual</em>, William McCune"},{"label":"Historical Background","html":"Automated reasoning systems had powerful inference rules but often struggled with search explosion. Otter focused on practical control of that search.","link":null},{"label":"Core Idea","html":"The prover repeatedly selects a promising clause, generates consequences through inference rules, and uses indexing and simplification to keep the search tractable. The key mechanism is Given-clause resolution and paramodulation, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","link":null},{"label":"Long-Term Legacy","html":"Experts generally treat Otter as a workhorse system in automated theorem proving and formal reasoning. Its long-term legacy is practical given-clause search, equality reasoning, and proof automation that influenced later tools including Prover9.","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1990-otter-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"Otter 实用定理证明风格的核心是什么？","en":"What was central to Otter's practical theorem-proving style?"},"options":[{"zh":"结合归结与参数调解的受控 given-clause 搜索","en":"A controlled given-clause search with resolution and paramodulation"},{"zh":"只有固定的棋类开局库","en":"Only a fixed chess opening book"},{"zh":"神经图像分类器","en":"A neural image classifier"},{"zh":"随机文本生成器","en":"A random text generator"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"Otter 用归结式推理、等式推理、索引和搜索控制启发式来搜索一阶子句空间。","en":"Otter searched first-order clause spaces using resolution-style inference, equality reasoning, indexing, and search-control heuristics."},"sourceIds":["source-otter-3-3-reference-manual"],"assetIds":["asset-1990-otter-input-clauses"]}]}`

### milestone-1992-svm

- Kind: changed
- `date`
  - Legacy: `null`
  - Archive: `"1992"`
- `achievement.sources`
  - Legacy: `[{"type":{"en":"Paper","zh":"论文"},"label":{"en":"A Training Algorithm for Optimal Margin Classifiers","zh":"最优间隔分类器的训练算法"},"url":"https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/130385.130401"},{"type":{"en":"Toolkit","zh":"工具包"},"label":{"en":"LIBSVM project page","zh":"LIBSVM project page"},"url":"https://www.csie.ntu.edu.tw/~cjlin/libsvm/"},{"type":{"en":"Model docs","zh":"模型文档"},"label":{"en":"scikit-learn SVC","zh":"scikit-learn SVC"},"url":"https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.svm.SVC.html"}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"source-a-training-algorithm-for-optimal-margin-classifiers","type":{"zh":"论文","en":"Paper"},"label":{"zh":"最优间隔分类器的训练算法","en":"A Training Algorithm for Optimal Margin Classifiers"},"url":"https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/130385.130401","reliability":"primary","sourceType":"paper"},{"id":"source-libsvm-project-page","type":{"zh":"工具包","en":"Toolkit"},"label":{"zh":"LIBSVM project page","en":"LIBSVM project page"},"url":"https://www.csie.ntu.edu.tw/~cjlin/libsvm/","reliability":"secondary","sourceType":"project-page"},{"id":"source-scikit-learn-svc","type":{"zh":"模型文档","en":"Model docs"},"label":{"zh":"scikit-learn SVC","en":"scikit-learn SVC"},"url":"https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.svm.SVC.html","reliability":"reference-only","sourceType":"reference-only"}]`
- `commentarySections`
  - Legacy: `[{"label":{"en":"Historical Background","zh":"历史背景"},"html":{"en":"In the early 1990s, machine learning needed methods that could generalize from limited data with clear mathematical guarantees. SVMs translated VC-style capacity control into a practical classifier.","zh":"20 世纪 90 年代初，机器学习需要能在有限数据上泛化并具有清晰数学保证的方法。SVM 将 VC 式容量控制转化为实用分类器。"}},{"label":{"en":"Core Idea","zh":"核心思想"},"html":{"en":"SVMs prefer a boundary that is not merely correct on training data, but comfortably separated from the closest examples. Kernels make nonlinear boundaries possible while preserving a convex optimization problem.","zh":"SVM 偏好不只是训练集正确、而且离最近样本足够远的边界。核函数让非线性边界成为可能，同时保留凸优化问题。"}},{"label":{"en":"Long-Term Legacy","zh":"长期影响"},"html":{"en":"Experts generally treat SVMs as mature classical methods whose conceptual importance exceeds their role in most modern deep-learning pipelines. Their long-term legacy is the margin-and-kernel view of generalization, robust boundaries, and nonlinear classification.","zh":"专家通常把 SVM 视为成熟的经典方法，其概念重要性超过了它在多数现代深度学习流水线中的实际角色。它的长期影响在于用间隔和核方法理解泛化、稳健边界和非线性分类。"}}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"historical-background","label":{"zh":"历史背景","en":"Historical Background"},"html":{"zh":"20 世纪 90 年代初，机器学习需要能在有限数据上泛化并具有清晰数学保证的方法。SVM 将 VC 式容量控制转化为实用分类器。","en":"In the early 1990s, machine learning needed methods that could generalize from limited data with clear mathematical guarantees. SVMs translated VC-style capacity control into a practical classifier."},"sourceIds":["source-a-training-algorithm-for-optimal-margin-classifiers"]},{"id":"core-idea","label":{"zh":"核心思想","en":"Core Idea"},"html":{"zh":"SVM 偏好不只是训练集正确、而且离最近样本足够远的边界。核函数让非线性边界成为可能，同时保留凸优化问题。","en":"SVMs prefer a boundary that is not merely correct on training data, but comfortably separated from the closest examples. Kernels make nonlinear boundaries possible while preserving a convex optimization problem."},"sourceIds":["source-a-training-algorithm-for-optimal-margin-classifiers"]},{"id":"long-term-legacy","label":{"zh":"长期影响","en":"Long-Term Legacy"},"html":{"zh":"专家通常把 SVM 视为成熟的经典方法，其概念重要性超过了它在多数现代深度学习流水线中的实际角色。它的长期影响在于用间隔和核方法理解泛化、稳健边界和非线性分类。","en":"Experts generally treat SVMs as mature classical methods whose conceptual importance exceeds their role in most modern deep-learning pipelines. Their long-term legacy is the margin-and-kernel view of generalization, robust boundaries, and nonlinear classification."},"sourceIds":["source-a-training-algorithm-for-optimal-margin-classifiers"]}]`
- `quizzes`
  - Legacy: `[{"id":"1992-svm-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What boundary does an SVM prefer?","zh":"SVM 偏好什么样的分类边界？"},"options":[{"en":"A boundary with the largest margin to nearby examples","zh":"与附近样本间隔最大的边界"},{"en":"A boundary chosen by image brightness","zh":"按图像亮度选择的边界"},{"en":"A boundary that ignores all labels","zh":"忽略所有标签的边界"},{"en":"A random line with no objective","zh":"没有目标函数的随机直线"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"The maximum-margin principle is the core of SVM classification and links the method to statistical learning theory.","zh":"最大间隔原则是 SVM 分类的核心，也把它与统计学习理论连接起来。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["machine-learning","classification"]}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"1992-svm-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"SVM 偏好什么样的分类边界？","en":"What boundary does an SVM prefer?"},"options":[{"zh":"与附近样本间隔最大的边界","en":"A boundary with the largest margin to nearby examples"},{"zh":"按图像亮度选择的边界","en":"A boundary chosen by image brightness"},{"zh":"忽略所有标签的边界","en":"A boundary that ignores all labels"},{"zh":"没有目标函数的随机直线","en":"A random line with no objective"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"最大间隔原则是 SVM 分类的核心，也把它与统计学习理论连接起来。","en":"The maximum-margin principle is the core of SVM classification and links the method to statistical learning theory."},"sourceIds":["source-a-training-algorithm-for-optimal-margin-classifiers"],"assetIds":["asset-1992-svm-isabelle-guyon"]}]`
- `viewModel.zh`
  - Legacy: `{"year":"1992","title":"支持向量机","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就","subtitle":"AI 百大成就","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>支持向量机让统计学习理论变得可操作：选择能以最大间隔分开类别的边界，再用核函数在变换后的特征空间中实现线性分离。它在深度学习复兴之前长期是主流机器学习工具。SVM 将优雅理论、凸优化和实用分类器连接在一起。</p>","figures":["","",""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/photos/1992-svm_isabelle-guyon.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1992-svm_max-margin.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1992-svm_kernel-lift.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"最优间隔分类器\"","quoteAttribution":"《最优间隔分类器的训练算法》, 伯恩哈德·E·博瑟、伊莎贝尔·M·居永、弗拉基米尔·N·瓦普尼克, 第五届计算学习理论年度研讨会, 1992","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100：统计学习 / 支持向量机","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"最优间隔分类器\"<br>source：《最优间隔分类器的训练算法》, 伯恩哈德·E·博瑟、伊莎贝尔·M·居永、弗拉基米尔·N·瓦普尼克, 第五届计算学习理论年度研讨会, 1992"},{"label":"历史背景","html":"20 世纪 90 年代初，机器学习需要能在有限数据上泛化并具有清晰数学保证的方法。SVM 将 VC 式容量控制转化为实用分类器。","link":null},{"label":"核心思想","html":"SVM 偏好不只是训练集正确、而且离最近样本足够远的边界。核函数让非线性边界成为可能，同时保留凸优化问题。","link":null},{"label":"长期影响","html":"专家通常把 SVM 视为成熟的经典方法，其概念重要性超过了它在多数现代深度学习流水线中的实际角色。它的长期影响在于用间隔和核方法理解泛化、稳健边界和非线性分类。","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1992-svm-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What boundary does an SVM prefer?","zh":"SVM 偏好什么样的分类边界？"},"options":[{"en":"A boundary with the largest margin to nearby examples","zh":"与附近样本间隔最大的边界"},{"en":"A boundary chosen by image brightness","zh":"按图像亮度选择的边界"},{"en":"A boundary that ignores all labels","zh":"忽略所有标签的边界"},{"en":"A random line with no objective","zh":"没有目标函数的随机直线"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"The maximum-margin principle is the core of SVM classification and links the method to statistical learning theory.","zh":"最大间隔原则是 SVM 分类的核心，也把它与统计学习理论连接起来。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["machine-learning","classification"]}]}`
  - Archive: `{"year":"1992","title":"支持向量机","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就","subtitle":"AI 百大成就","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>支持向量机让统计学习理论变得可操作：选择能以最大间隔分开类别的边界，再用核函数在变换后的特征空间中实现线性分离。它在深度学习复兴之前长期是主流机器学习工具。SVM 将优雅理论、凸优化和实用分类器连接在一起。</p>","figures":["","",""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/photos/1992-svm_isabelle-guyon.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1992-svm_max-margin.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1992-svm_kernel-lift.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"最优间隔分类器\"","quoteAttribution":"《最优间隔分类器的训练算法》, 伯恩哈德·E·博瑟、伊莎贝尔·M·居永、弗拉基米尔·N·瓦普尼克, 第五届计算学习理论年度研讨会, 1992","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100：统计学习 / 支持向量机","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"最优间隔分类器\"<br>source：《最优间隔分类器的训练算法》, 伯恩哈德·E·博瑟、伊莎贝尔·M·居永、弗拉基米尔·N·瓦普尼克, 第五届计算学习理论年度研讨会, 1992"},{"label":"历史背景","html":"20 世纪 90 年代初，机器学习需要能在有限数据上泛化并具有清晰数学保证的方法。SVM 将 VC 式容量控制转化为实用分类器。","link":null},{"label":"核心思想","html":"SVM 偏好不只是训练集正确、而且离最近样本足够远的边界。核函数让非线性边界成为可能，同时保留凸优化问题。","link":null},{"label":"长期影响","html":"专家通常把 SVM 视为成熟的经典方法，其概念重要性超过了它在多数现代深度学习流水线中的实际角色。它的长期影响在于用间隔和核方法理解泛化、稳健边界和非线性分类。","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1992-svm-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"SVM 偏好什么样的分类边界？","en":"What boundary does an SVM prefer?"},"options":[{"zh":"与附近样本间隔最大的边界","en":"A boundary with the largest margin to nearby examples"},{"zh":"按图像亮度选择的边界","en":"A boundary chosen by image brightness"},{"zh":"忽略所有标签的边界","en":"A boundary that ignores all labels"},{"zh":"没有目标函数的随机直线","en":"A random line with no objective"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"最大间隔原则是 SVM 分类的核心，也把它与统计学习理论连接起来。","en":"The maximum-margin principle is the core of SVM classification and links the method to statistical learning theory."},"sourceIds":["source-a-training-algorithm-for-optimal-margin-classifiers"],"assetIds":["asset-1992-svm-isabelle-guyon"]}]}`
- `viewModel.en`
  - Legacy: `{"year":"1992","title":"Support Vector Machines","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 Achievements","subtitle":"AI Top 100 Achievements","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>SVMs made statistical learning theory tangible: choose a boundary that separates classes with the largest margin, then use kernels to make linear separation possible in transformed feature spaces. The method became a dominant machine-learning tool before deep learning’s resurgence. It connected elegant theory, convex optimization, and practical classifiers.</p>","figures":["","",""],"photos":["resources/images/bench-council-ai100/photos/1992-svm_isabelle-guyon.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1992-svm_max-margin.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1992-svm_kernel-lift.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"optimal margin classifiers\"","quoteAttribution":"<em>A Training Algorithm for Optimal Margin Classifiers</em>, Bernhard E. SVMs translated VC-style capacity control into a practical classifier.","link":null},{"label":"Core Idea","html":"SVMs prefer a boundary that is not merely correct on training data, but comfortably separated from the closest examples. Kernels make nonlinear boundaries possible while preserving a convex optimization problem.","link":null},{"label":"Long-Term Legacy","html":"Experts generally treat SVMs as mature classical methods whose conceptual importance exceeds their role in most modern deep-learning pipelines. Their long-term legacy is the margin-and-kernel view of generalization, robust boundaries, and nonlinear classification.","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1992-svm-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What boundary does an SVM prefer?","zh":"SVM 偏好什么样的分类边界？"},"options":[{"en":"A boundary with the largest margin to nearby examples","zh":"与附近样本间隔最大的边界"},{"en":"A boundary chosen by image brightness","zh":"按图像亮度选择的边界"},{"en":"A boundary that ignores all labels","zh":"忽略所有标签的边界"},{"en":"A random line with no objective","zh":"没有目标函数的随机直线"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"The maximum-margin principle is the core of SVM classification and links the method to statistical learning theory.","zh":"最大间隔原则是 SVM 分类的核心，也把它与统计学习理论连接起来。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["machine-learning","classification"]}]}`

### milestone-1994-chinook

- Kind: changed
- `date`
  - Legacy: `null`
  - Archive: `"1989-2007"`
- `achievement.sources`
  - Legacy: `[{"type":{"en":"Official page","zh":"官方页面"},"label":{"en":"Chinook project home","zh":"Chinook 项目主页"},"url":"https://webdocs.cs.ualberta.ca/~chinook/index.php"},{"type":{"en":"Paper","zh":"论文"},"label":{"en":"Checkers Is Solved","zh":"跳棋已被求解"},"url":"https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.1144079"},{"type":{"en":"Publications","zh":"论文列表"},"label":{"en":"Chinook publications","zh":"Chinook 论文列表"},"url":"https://webdocs.cs.ualberta.ca/~chinook/publications/"},{"type":{"en":"Image source","zh":"图片来源"},"label":{"en":"Jonathan Schaeffer portrait","zh":"乔纳森·谢弗 人物照片"},"url":"https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Jonathan_Schaeffer.jpg"}]`

- `commentarySections`
  - Legacy: `[{"label":{"en":"Historical Background","zh":"历史背景"},"html":{"en":"Checkers was complex enough to challenge search algorithms but structured enough for decades of endgame database construction. This context helps viewers place Chinook in the technical problems and research priorities of its time.","zh":"跳棋足够复杂，可以挑战搜索算法；同时结构足够明确，适合长期构建残局数据库。 这段背景帮助观众把Chinook 跳棋程序放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。"}},{"label":{"en":"Core Idea","zh":"核心思想"},"html":{"en":"Chinook paired forward search with solved endgame tables, letting the program connect current choices to proven late-game outcomes. The key mechanism is Alpha-beta search plus endgame databases, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","zh":"Chinook 将前向搜索与已求解残局表结合，使程序能把当前选择连接到被证明的终局结果。 关键机制是alpha-beta 搜索加残局数据库，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。"}},{"label":{"en":"Long-Term Legacy","zh":"长期影响"},"html":{"en":"Experts generally treat Chinook as a landmark in game-solving research and high-performance search. Its long-term legacy is showing how expert evaluation, alpha-beta search, opening knowledge, and endgame databases can combine to approach perfect play.","zh":"专家通常把 Chinook 视为游戏求解研究和高性能搜索的里程碑。它的长期影响在于展示专家评估、alpha-beta 搜索、开局知识和残局数据库如何结合并逼近完美对弈。"}}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"historical-background","label":{"zh":"历史背景","en":"Historical Background"},"html":{"zh":"跳棋足够复杂，可以挑战搜索算法；同时结构足够明确，适合长期构建残局数据库。 这段背景帮助观众把Chinook 跳棋程序放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。","en":"Checkers was complex enough to challenge search algorithms but structured enough for decades of endgame database construction. This context helps viewers place Chinook in the technical problems and research priorities of its time."},"sourceIds":["source-chinook-project-home"]},{"id":"core-idea","label":{"zh":"核心思想","en":"Core Idea"},"html":{"zh":"Chinook 将前向搜索与已求解残局表结合，使程序能把当前选择连接到被证明的终局结果。 关键机制是alpha-beta 搜索加残局数据库，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","en":"Chinook paired forward search with solved endgame tables, letting the program connect current choices to proven late-game outcomes. The key mechanism is Alpha-beta search plus endgame databases, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior."},"sourceIds":["source-chinook-project-home"]},{"id":"long-term-legacy","label":{"zh":"长期影响","en":"Long-Term Legacy"},"html":{"zh":"专家通常把 Chinook 视为游戏求解研究和高性能搜索的里程碑。它的长期影响在于展示专家评估、alpha-beta 搜索、开局知识和残局数据库如何结合并逼近完美对弈。","en":"Experts generally treat Chinook as a landmark in game-solving research and high-performance search. Its long-term legacy is showing how expert evaluation, alpha-beta search, opening knowledge, and endgame databases can combine to approach perfect play."},"sourceIds":["source-chinook-project-home"]}]`
- `quizzes`
  - Legacy: `[{"id":"1994-chinook-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What did Chinook eventually prove about checkers?","zh":"Chinook 最终证明了跳棋的什么性质？"},"options":[{"en":"Perfect play from both sides leads to a draw","zh":"双方完美对弈会导致和棋"},{"en":"Every game is won by the first move","zh":"每局都由第一步直接获胜"},{"en":"Search is unnecessary in games","zh":"游戏中不需要搜索"},{"en":"Only language models can play checkers","zh":"只有语言模型能下跳棋"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"The Chinook team used search and endgame databases to weakly solve checkers as a draw under perfect play.","zh":"Chinook 团队用搜索和残局数据库弱求解跳棋，证明完美对弈下为和棋。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["game-ai","search"]}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"1994-chinook-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"Chinook 最终证明了跳棋的什么性质？","en":"What did Chinook eventually prove about checkers?"},"options":[{"zh":"双方完美对弈会导致和棋","en":"Perfect play from both sides leads to a draw"},{"zh":"每局都由第一步直接获胜","en":"Every game is won by the first move"},{"zh":"游戏中不需要搜索","en":"Search is unnecessary in games"},{"zh":"只有语言模型能下跳棋","en":"Only language models can play checkers"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"Chinook 团队用搜索和残局数据库弱求解跳棋，证明完美对弈下为和棋。","en":"The Chinook team used search and endgame databases to weakly solve checkers as a draw under perfect play."},"sourceIds":["source-chinook-project-home"],"assetIds":["asset-1994-chinook-homepage"]}]`
- `viewModel.zh`
  - Legacy: `{"year":"1989-2007","title":"Chinook 跳棋程序","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就","subtitle":"AI 百大成就","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>Chinook 把游戏 AI 从竞技对弈推进到数学求解。阿尔伯塔大学团队结合开局库、深层 alpha-beta 搜索、专家评估和庞大残局数据库，最终证明跳棋在完美对弈下结果为和棋。</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0d/Jonathan_Schaeffer.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/source/1994-chinook_homepage.png","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1994-chinook_endgame-database.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1994-chinook_perfect-play.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"跳棋完美对弈结果为和棋\"","quoteAttribution":"《跳棋已被求解》, 乔纳森·谢弗等","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100：游戏 / Chinook 跳棋程序","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"跳棋完美对弈结果为和棋\"<br>source：《跳棋已被求解》, 乔纳森·谢弗等"},{"label":"历史背景","html":"跳棋足够复杂，可以挑战搜索算法；同时结构足够明确，适合长期构建残局数据库。 这段背景帮助观众把Chinook 跳棋程序放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。","link":null},{"label":"核心思想","html":"Chinook 将前向搜索与已求解残局表结合，使程序能把当前选择连接到被证明的终局结果。 关键机制是alpha-beta 搜索加残局数据库，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","link":null},{"label":"长期影响","html":"专家通常把 Chinook 视为游戏求解研究和高性能搜索的里程碑。它的长期影响在于展示专家评估、alpha-beta 搜索、开局知识和残局数据库如何结合并逼近完美对弈。","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1994-chinook-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What did Chinook eventually prove about checkers?","zh":"Chinook 最终证明了跳棋的什么性质？"},"options":[{"en":"Perfect play from both sides leads to a draw","zh":"双方完美对弈会导致和棋"},{"en":"Every game is won by the first move","zh":"每局都由第一步直接获胜"},{"en":"Search is unnecessary in games","zh":"游戏中不需要搜索"},{"en":"Only language models can play checkers","zh":"只有语言模型能下跳棋"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"The Chinook team used search and endgame databases to weakly solve checkers as a draw under perfect play.","zh":"Chinook 团队用搜索和残局数据库弱求解跳棋，证明完美对弈下为和棋。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["game-ai","search"]}]}`
  - Archive: `{"year":"1989-2007","title":"Chinook 跳棋程序","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就","subtitle":"AI 百大成就","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>Chinook 把游戏 AI 从竞技对弈推进到数学求解。阿尔伯塔大学团队结合开局库、深层 alpha-beta 搜索、专家评估和庞大残局数据库，最终证明跳棋在完美对弈下结果为和棋。</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0d/Jonathan_Schaeffer.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/source/1994-chinook_homepage.png","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1994-chinook_endgame-database.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1994-chinook_perfect-play.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"跳棋完美对弈结果为和棋\"","quoteAttribution":"《跳棋已被求解》, 乔纳森·谢弗等","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100：游戏 / Chinook 跳棋程序","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"跳棋完美对弈结果为和棋\"<br>source：《跳棋已被求解》, 乔纳森·谢弗等"},{"label":"历史背景","html":"跳棋足够复杂，可以挑战搜索算法；同时结构足够明确，适合长期构建残局数据库。 这段背景帮助观众把Chinook 跳棋程序放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。","link":null},{"label":"核心思想","html":"Chinook 将前向搜索与已求解残局表结合，使程序能把当前选择连接到被证明的终局结果。 关键机制是alpha-beta 搜索加残局数据库，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","link":null},{"label":"长期影响","html":"专家通常把 Chinook 视为游戏求解研究和高性能搜索的里程碑。它的长期影响在于展示专家评估、alpha-beta 搜索、开局知识和残局数据库如何结合并逼近完美对弈。","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1994-chinook-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"Chinook 最终证明了跳棋的什么性质？","en":"What did Chinook eventually prove about checkers?"},"options":[{"zh":"双方完美对弈会导致和棋","en":"Perfect play from both sides leads to a draw"},{"zh":"每局都由第一步直接获胜","en":"Every game is won by the first move"},{"zh":"游戏中不需要搜索","en":"Search is unnecessary in games"},{"zh":"只有语言模型能下跳棋","en":"Only language models can play checkers"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"Chinook 团队用搜索和残局数据库弱求解跳棋，证明完美对弈下为和棋。","en":"The Chinook team used search and endgame databases to weakly solve checkers as a draw under perfect play."},"sourceIds":["source-chinook-project-home"],"assetIds":["asset-1994-chinook-homepage"]}]}`

  - Legacy: `{"year":"1989-2007","title":"Chinook","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 Achievements","subtitle":"AI Top 100 Achievements","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>Chinook pushed game AI from competitive play toward mathematical solution. The University of Alberta team combined opening books, deep alpha-beta search, expert evaluation, and enormous endgame databases, eventually proving that perfect play in checkers leads to a draw.</p>","figures":[""],"photos":["https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0d/Jonathan_Schaeffer.jpg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/source/1994-chinook_homepage.png","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1994-chinook_endgame-database.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1994-chinook_perfect-play.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"Perfect play in checkers is a draw\"","quoteAttribution":"<em>Checkers Is Solved</em>, Jonathan Schaeffer et al.","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100: Game / Chinook","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"Perfect play in checkers is a draw\"<br>source：<em>Checkers Is Solved</em>, Jonathan Schaeffer et al."},{"label":"Historical Background","html":"Checkers was complex enough to challenge search algorithms but structured enough for decades of endgame database construction. ### milestone-1996-dbscan

- Kind: changed
- `date`
  - Legacy: `null`
  - Archive: `"1996"`
- `achievement.sources`
  - Legacy: `[{"type":{"en":"Paper","zh":"论文"},"label":{"en":"A density-based algorithm for discovering clusters","zh":"一种发现簇的基于密度的算法"},"url":"https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.5555/3001460.3001507"},{"type":{"en":"Retrospective","zh":"回顾"},"label":{"en":"DBSCAN Revisited, Revisited","zh":"再访 DBSCAN，再访"},"url":"https://doi.org/10.1145/3068335"},{"type":{"en":"Documentation","zh":"文档"},"label":{"en":"scikit-learn DBSCAN documentation","zh":"scikit-learn DBSCAN 文档"},"url":"https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/clustering.html#dbscan"},{"type":{"en":"Image source","zh":"图片来源"},"label":{"en":"Martin Ester portrait","zh":"马丁·埃斯特 人物照片"},"url":"https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:SFU_Professor_Martin_Ester_2019.jpg"}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"source-a-density-based-algorithm-for-discovering-clusters","type":{"zh":"论文","en":"Paper"},"label":{"zh":"一种发现簇的基于密度的算法","en":"A density-based algorithm for discovering clusters"},"url":"https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.5555/3001460.3001507","reliability":"primary","sourceType":"paper"},{"id":"source-dbscan-revisited-revisited","type":{"zh":"回顾","en":"Retrospective"},"label":{"zh":"再访 DBSCAN，再访","en":"DBSCAN Revisited, Revisited"},"url":"https://doi.org/10.1145/3068335","reliability":"reference-only","sourceType":"reference-only"},{"id":"source-scikit-learn-dbscan-documentation","type":{"zh":"文档","en":"Documentation"},"label":{"zh":"scikit-learn DBSCAN 文档","en":"scikit-learn DBSCAN documentation"},"url":"https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/clustering.html#dbscan","reliability":"reference-only","sourceType":"reference-only"},{"id":"source-martin-ester-portrait","type":{"zh":"图片来源","en":"Image source"},"label":{"zh":"马丁·埃斯特 人物照片","en":"Martin Ester portrait"},"url":"https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:SFU_Professor_Martin_Ester_2019.jpg","reliability":"reference-only","sourceType":"reference-only"}]`
- `commentarySections`
  - Legacy: `[{"label":{"en":"Historical Background","zh":"历史背景"},"html":{"en":"Many clustering methods preferred round clusters and required the number of clusters before analysis. This context helps viewers place DBSCAN in the technical problems and research priorities of its time.","zh":"许多聚类方法偏好圆形簇，并要求分析前指定簇数量。 这段背景帮助观众把DBSCAN 密度聚类放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。"}},{"label":{"en":"Core Idea","zh":"核心思想"},"html":{"en":"A point with enough neighbors within epsilon is a core point; clusters expand through chains of density reachability. The key mechanism is Density reachability with epsilon and MinPts, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","zh":"如果一个点在 epsilon 范围内有足够邻居，它就是核心点；簇通过密度可达链条扩展。 关键机制是基于 epsilon 与 MinPts 的密度可达，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。"}},{"label":{"en":"Long-Term Legacy","zh":"长期影响"},"html":{"en":"Experts generally treat DBSCAN as a standard density-based clustering method, especially for spatial data and irregular cluster shapes. Its long-term legacy is the core-border-noise vocabulary that made outlier handling and arbitrary-shape clusters practical.","zh":"专家通常把 DBSCAN 视为标准的基于密度聚类方法，尤其适用于空间数据和不规则簇形状。它的长期影响在于核心点、边界点和噪声点的语言让异常处理和任意形状簇变得实用。"}}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"historical-background","label":{"zh":"历史背景","en":"Historical Background"},"html":{"zh":"许多聚类方法偏好圆形簇，并要求分析前指定簇数量。 这段背景帮助观众把DBSCAN 密度聚类放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。","en":"Many clustering methods preferred round clusters and required the number of clusters before analysis. This context helps viewers place DBSCAN in the technical problems and research priorities of its time."},"sourceIds":["source-a-density-based-algorithm-for-discovering-clusters"]},{"id":"core-idea","label":{"zh":"核心思想","en":"Core Idea"},"html":{"zh":"如果一个点在 epsilon 范围内有足够邻居，它就是核心点；簇通过密度可达链条扩展。 关键机制是基于 epsilon 与 MinPts 的密度可达，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","en":"A point with enough neighbors within epsilon is a core point; clusters expand through chains of density reachability. The key mechanism is Density reachability with epsilon and MinPts, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior."},"sourceIds":["source-a-density-based-algorithm-for-discovering-clusters"]},{"id":"long-term-legacy","label":{"zh":"长期影响","en":"Long-Term Legacy"},"html":{"zh":"专家通常把 DBSCAN 视为标准的基于密度聚类方法，尤其适用于空间数据和不规则簇形状。它的长期影响在于核心点、边界点和噪声点的语言让异常处理和任意形状簇变得实用。","en":"Experts generally treat DBSCAN as a standard density-based clustering method, especially for spatial data and irregular cluster shapes. Its long-term legacy is the core-border-noise vocabulary that made outlier handling and arbitrary-shape clusters practical."},"sourceIds":["source-a-density-based-algorithm-for-discovering-clusters"]}]`
- `quizzes`
  - Legacy: `[{"id":"1996-dbscan-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What does DBSCAN do with low-density points?","zh":"DBSCAN 如何处理低密度点？"},"options":[{"en":"It can label them as noise instead of forcing them into a cluster","zh":"它可以把它们标为噪声，而不是强行归入簇"},{"en":"It always creates one cluster per point","zh":"它总是为每个点创建一个簇"},{"en":"It turns them into theorem clauses","zh":"它把它们变成定理子句"},{"en":"It uses a chess opening book","zh":"它使用棋类开局库"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"DBSCAN forms clusters from dense neighborhoods and can leave sparse outliers outside the clusters.","zh":"DBSCAN 从密集邻域形成簇，并可把稀疏离群点留在簇外。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["clustering","density"]}]`
  - Archive: `[{"id":"1996-dbscan-quiz-1","storylineId":"","question":{"zh":"DBSCAN 如何处理低密度点？","en":"What does DBSCAN do with low-density points?"},"options":[{"zh":"它可以把它们标为噪声，而不是强行归入簇","en":"It can label them as noise instead of forcing them into a cluster"},{"zh":"它总是为每个点创建一个簇","en":"It always creates one cluster per point"},{"zh":"它把它们变成定理子句","en":"It turns them into theorem clauses"},{"zh":"它使用棋类开局库","en":"It uses a chess opening book"}],"answerIndex":0,"answer":0,"explanation":{"zh":"DBSCAN 从密集邻域形成簇，并可把稀疏离群点留在簇外。","en":"DBSCAN forms clusters from dense neighborhoods and can leave sparse outliers outside the clusters."},"sourceIds":["source-a-density-based-algorithm-for-discovering-clusters"],"assetIds":["asset-1996-dbscan-density-reachability"]}]`
- `viewModel.zh`
  - Legacy: `{"year":"1996","title":"DBSCAN 密度聚类","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就","subtitle":"AI 百大成就","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>DBSCAN 改变聚类方式：它询问数据哪里足够密集，而不是预先指定有多少簇。它从核心点生长簇，连接密度可达邻域，并把稀疏点标为噪声，因此适合不规则空间模式。</p>","figures":["",""],"photos":["https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/4/41/SFU_Professor_Martin_Ester_2019.jpg","https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/05/DBSCAN-density-data.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1996-dbscan_density-reachability.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1996-dbscan_noise-core-border.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"簇是被稀疏噪声隔开的密集区域\"","quoteAttribution":"《一种在含噪大型空间数据库中发现簇的基于密度的算法》, 马丁·埃斯特, 汉斯-彼得·克里格尔, 约尔格·桑德, 徐晓伟","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100：聚类、分类与回归 / DBSCAN 密度聚类","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"簇是被稀疏噪声隔开的密集区域\"<br>source：《一种在含噪大型空间数据库中发现簇的基于密度的算法》, 马丁·埃斯特, 汉斯-彼得·克里格尔, 约尔格·桑德, 徐晓伟"},{"label":"历史背景","html":"许多聚类方法偏好圆形簇，并要求分析前指定簇数量。 这段背景帮助观众把DBSCAN 密度聚类放回当时的技术问题和研究重点中理解。","link":null},{"label":"核心思想","html":"如果一个点在 epsilon 范围内有足够邻居，它就是核心点；簇通过密度可达链条扩展。 关键机制是基于 epsilon 与 MinPts 的密度可达，它把资料线索与可见的演示行为连接起来。","link":null},{"label":"长期影响","html":"专家通常把 DBSCAN 视为标准的基于密度聚类方法，尤其适用于空间数据和不规则簇形状。它的长期影响在于核心点、边界点和噪声点的语言让异常处理和任意形状簇变得实用。","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1996-dbscan-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What does DBSCAN do with low-density points?","zh":"DBSCAN 如何处理低密度点？"},"options":[{"en":"It can label them as noise instead of forcing them into a cluster","zh":"它可以把它们标为噪声，而不是强行归入簇"},{"en":"It always creates one cluster per point","zh":"它总是为每个点创建一个簇"},{"en":"It turns them into theorem clauses","zh":"它把它们变成定理子句"},{"en":"It uses a chess opening book","zh":"它使用棋类开局库"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"DBSCAN forms clusters from dense neighborhoods and can leave sparse outliers outside the clusters.","zh":"DBSCAN 从密集邻域形成簇，并可把稀疏离群点留在簇外。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["clustering","density"]}]}`

- `viewModel.en`
  - Legacy: `{"year":"1996","title":"DBSCAN","category":"BenchCouncil AI100 Achievements","subtitle":"AI Top 100 Achievements","location":"","descriptionHtml":"<p>DBSCAN changed clustering by asking where data are dense rather than how many clusters should be declared in advance. It grows clusters from core points, connects density-reachable neighborhoods, and labels sparse points as noise, making it useful for irregular spatial patterns.</p>","figures":["",""],"photos":["https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/4/41/SFU_Professor_Martin_Ester_2019.jpg","https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/05/DBSCAN-density-data.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1996-dbscan_density-reachability.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1996-dbscan_noise-core-border.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"Clusters are dense regions separated by sparse noise\"","quoteAttribution":"<em>A density-based algorithm for discovering clusters in large spatial databases with noise</em>, Martin Ester, Hans-Peter Kriegel, Jorg Sander, Xiaowei Xu","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100: Cluster, Classification, Regression / DBSCAN","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"Clusters are dense regions separated by sparse noise\"<br>source：<em>A density-based algorithm for discovering clusters in large spatial databases with noise</em>, Martin Ester, Hans-Peter Kriegel, Jorg Sander, Xiaowei Xu"},{"label":"Historical Background","html":"Many clustering methods preferred round clusters and required the number of clusters before analysis. This context helps viewers place DBSCAN in the technical problems and research priorities of its time.","link":null},{"label":"Core Idea","html":"A point with enough neighbors within epsilon is a core point; clusters expand through chains of density reachability. The key mechanism is Density reachability with epsilon and MinPts, which links the source material to the visible demo behavior.","link":null},{"label":"Long-Term Legacy","html":"Experts generally treat DBSCAN as a standard density-based clustering method, especially for spatial data and irregular cluster shapes. Its long-term legacy is the core-border-noise vocabulary that made outlier handling and arbitrary-shape clusters practical.","link":null}],"quizzes":[{"id":"1996-dbscan-quiz-1","question":{"en":"What does DBSCAN do with low-density points?","zh":"DBSCAN 如何处理低密度点？"},"options":[{"en":"It can label them as noise instead of forcing them into a cluster","zh":"它可以把它们标为噪声，而不是强行归入簇"},{"en":"It always creates one cluster per point","zh":"它总是为每个点创建一个簇"},{"en":"It turns them into theorem clauses","zh":"它把它们变成定理子句"},{"en":"It uses a chess opening book","zh":"它使用棋类开局库"}],"answerIndex":0,"explanation":{"en":"DBSCAN forms clusters from dense neighborhoods and can leave sparse outliers outside the clusters.","zh":"DBSCAN 从密集邻域形成簇，并可把稀疏离群点留在簇外。"},"source":{"label":{"en":"BenchCouncil AI100 achievement notes","zh":"BenchCouncil AI100 成就内容"}},"tags":["clustering","density"]}]}`

It grows clusters from core points, connects density-reachable neighborhoods, and labels sparse points as noise, making it useful for irregular spatial patterns.</p>","figures":["",""],"photos":["https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/4/41/SFU_Professor_Martin_Ester_2019.jpg","https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/05/DBSCAN-density-data.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1996-dbscan_density-reachability.svg","resources/images/bench-council-ai100/explainers/1996-dbscan_noise-core-border.svg"],"primaryVideo":null,"quoteHtml":"\"Clusters are dense regions separated by sparse noise\"","quoteAttribution":"<em>A density-based algorithm for discovering clusters in large spatial databases with noise</em>, Martin Ester, Hans-Peter Kriegel, Jorg Sander, Xiaowei Xu","quoteLabel":"","quotePage":"BenchCouncil AI100: Cluster, Classification, Regression / DBSCAN","commentarySections":[{"label":"quoteExcerpt","html":"\"Clusters are dense regions separated by sparse noise\"<br>source：<em>A density-based algorithm for discovering clusters in large spatial databases with noise</em>, Martin Ester, Hans-Peter Kriegel, Jorg Sander, Xiaowei Xu"},{"label":"Historical Background","html":"Many clustering methods preferred round clusters and required the number of clusters before analysis. This context helps viewers place DBSCAN in the technical problems and research priorities of its time.","link":null},{"label":"Core Idea","html":"A point with enough neighbors within epsilon is a core point; clusters expand through chains of density reachability.
